captions: Scored 15/100 — Financially Fragile. My credit cards aren't just in my wallet, they're basically my roommates. 💳😭
15 out of 100. My credit card is co-signing my lifestyle at 21% interest and honestly? It has more faith in me than I do. 💀
Started from a 15/100 Financially Fragile. The only way out is up — and this time I'm not putting it on the card. 📈🔥
Scored 33/100: Financially Fragile. Turns out 'I'll pay it off next month' was less a plan and more a lifestyle. 😅
33/100 and $250 in savings while CC debt runs the show. I am Visa's favorite customer and I am not okay. 💳💀
33 today, but awareness is the first deposit. Rebuilding looks messy at the start — and that's still progress. 🌱
Scored 45/100. Turns out earning more doesn't automatically mean keeping more. Lesson learned the humbling way. 😅
45/100 and $50k in credit card debt on a $180k salary. The math isn't mathing, and I finally had to face it. 😬
My score said 'Surviving' and honestly? That's the push I needed to stop surviving and start actually thriving. 💪
Scored 51/100 💀 Got a house, got a job, and still out here putting $5 of gas in a Ferrari. The math ain't mathing bro.
51/100 and my credit card balance is literally the villain in my origin story. No notes. All facts. 😭📊
51 today. But the CC debt's getting slayed, the savings rate's going up, and the glow-up arc starts NOW. 📈🔥
Scored 87/100 on my financial health check 👀 Older me would NOT believe this.
87/100 — apparently my finances are 'almost unfairly healthy.' I'll take it. 💪
From stressing about every swipe to an 87. The glow-up is real and it's documented. 🌱
Scored 87/100 on my finances and somehow I'm still the problem 💅 at least the app said so.
87/100. Thriving. Meanwhile inflation is out here quietly nibbling my $80k like a little rat 🐀💀
Started budgeting as a joke. Ended up with an 87. The villain arc is officially a glow-up. 📈
